solve(N) ->
    % 1. Generate all sums X = a^3 + b^3 <= N
    AllSums = generate_sums(1, N, []),
    
    % 2. Sort the sums
    SortedSums = lists:sort(AllSums),
    
    % 3. Collect sums that appeared at least twice
    GoodSums = count_and_collect(SortedSums),
    
    % The sums are collected in reverse order of appearance in the sorted list, 
    % so we reverse the final result to maintain ascending order.
    lists:reverse(GoodSums).

%% Helper: Recursive generation of all sums
generate_sums(b, N, Sums) when b*b*b > N ->
    Sums;
generate_sums(b, N, Sums) ->
    B3 = b*b*b,
    
    % Generate sums for fixed b: [X1, X2, ...]
    Sums_for_b = generate_sums_for_b(1, b, N, B3, []),
    
    % Recurse for next b, prepending results
    generate_sums(b+1, N, [Sums_for_b | Sums]).

%% Helper: Generate sums for a fixed b
generate_sums_for_b(a, max_a, N, B3, Acc) when a > max_a ->
    Acc;
generate_sums_for_b(a, max_a, N, B3, Acc) ->
    A3 = a*a*a,
    X = A3 + B3,
    
    if X > N ->
        % Since a is increasing, X will only get larger, so stop inner loop
        Acc;
    true ->
        NewAcc = [X | Acc],
        generate_sums_for_b(a+1, max_a, N, B3, NewAcc)
    end.

%% Helper: Count occurrences of consecutive elements in a sorted list
count_and_collect(SortedList) ->
    count_and_collect_helper(SortedList, []).

%% Helper: Recursively traverses the sorted list, counting runs
count_and_collect_helper([], GoodSums) ->
    GoodSums;
count_and_collect_helper([H|T], GoodSums) ->
    % Find how many times H appears consecutively
    {Run, Remaining} = find_run(H, T),
    
    % Decide if H is good (Run >= 2)
    NewGoodSums = if Run >= 2 -> [H]; true -> [] end,
    
    % Continue recursion, appending new good sums to the front
    count_and_collect_helper(Remaining, NewGoodSums ++ GoodSums).

%% Helper: Finds the run length of element H in the tail T, and the rest of the list
find_run(H, T) ->
    find_run_helper(H, T, 1, T).

find_run_helper(_, [], Count, []) -> {Count, []};
find_run_helper(H, [T1|Rest], Count, Remaining) when H == T1 ->
    find_run_helper(H, Rest, Count + 1, Remaining);
find_run_helper(H, [T1|Rest], Count, Remaining) when H \= T1 ->
    {Count, [T1|Rest]}.
```
